title: Chief Operating Officer
description:
Start Up Hedge Fund

Purpose of the Position

Reporting to the Chief Executive Officer, the newly-created Chief Operating Officer position will oversee the company's operating engine. This individual will translate strategy into day to day execution, scale the distribution and client service capabilities, and build the infrastructure that propels the company's next stage of growth. This is a hands on leadership role for a builder who thrives in white space, wears multiple hats with ease, and is motivated by the chance to shape a company's future.

Key Responsibilities

Operational Leadership
- Collaborate with the Chief Executive Officer and other executives to shape and execute the firm's strategic roadmap, with a focus on scaling distribution and servicing RIAs.
- Develop and implement operational policies, processes, and systems to drive efficiency and support scaling.
- Oversee the Operations teams' day-to-day operational activities to ensure exceptional client experience and alignment with firm goals.
- Design and implement scalable operational frameworks and workflows.
- Continually improve and refine current frameworks as needed.
- Evaluate and oversee third-party service providers, including custodians, fund administrators, compliance vendors, and CRM/tech stack solutions.
- Identify and mitigate risks related to operations, compliance, and technology deployment.

Team Leadership & Culture
- Build, mentor, and inspire high performing teams across multiple functions, champion a culture of ownership, accountability, and excellence.
- Drive cross functional collaboration so that product, distribution, operations, and finance have alignment.
- Establish and manage KPIs that keep teams focused on the metrics that matter.

Risk Management & Compliance
- Implement robust operational risk management frameworks to protect investor capital and the firm's reputation.
- Partner with external vendors and internal counsel to ensure full compliance with SEC and other applicable regulations.

Financial Management
- Lead budgeting, forecasting, and financial planning processes.
- Deliver timely, accurate reporting to the CEO and Board—covering financials as well as KPIs.

Year one critical success factors
- The individual will have integrated effectively with the company team, developed a strong understanding of the business and built trust and respect with internal and relevant external stakeholders.
- The COO will have leveraged their business and strategic acumen to enhance the organizational structure and policies and brought strong professional leadership and management to their team.
- Built the trust of the company and its partners as an exceptional leader and as a transparent communicator, collaborative executive with a 'roll-up your sleeves' leadership style.

The Candidate

Experience and Professional Qualifications
- Deep RIA / asset management expertise— understand the operational nuances of serving advisors (optional).
- Proven scaler—track record of building infrastructure in an early stage or growth stage asset manager.
- Metrics driven mindset—fluent in translating strategy into operational, financial, and performance KPIs.
- Experience supporting capital raises.
- Highly collaborative, adaptable, and intellectually curious—comfortable navigating ambiguity and wearing multiple hats.
- Customer centric orientation and approachable communication style.
- Visionary leadership that balances strategic thinking with roll up your sleeves execution.

Education
- Must have an undergraduate degree.

Perks & Benefits
- Competitive compensation (salary + equity) aligned with experience and impact.
- Hybrid work arrangements.
- Budget for continued education and professional development.
- A mission driven, high trust team that values excellence, integrity, diverse perspectives, and genuine camaraderie.

title: Operating Partner – Enterprise Transformation
description:
Interim Operating Partner – Enterprise Transformation (US Mega Fund)

Location: United States (must be US-based or hold valid US work authorisation)
Duration: TBC
Day Rate: $3,000–$4,000+

Overview

We're supporting a US-based fund undergoing a deliberate shift in its approach to value creation across a complex, multi-entity portfolio.

This mandate requires a true enterprise-level operator, someone who has reported directly to the CEO, led large-scale transformation across $1B+ businesses, and can influence both management teams and investors.

The fund is moving toward strategic operators who can:
- Shape the value creation agenda
- Lead enterprise-wide change
- Work in the business, not just advise on it
- Translate strategy into operational and commercial results across multiple assets.

Key Priorities & Responsibilities:
- Partner with CEOs and senior leadership across portfolio companies to define, pressure-test, and execute enterprise-wide transformation plans.
- Lead large-scale commercial, operational, and organisational transformation across multi-entity or multi-product businesses.
- Serve as a trusted operating partner to the fund, advising on strategic priorities, sequencing, and value creation levers across assets.
- Identify and unlock top-line growth opportunities, including GTM redesign, pricing, commercial acceleration, channel expansion, or post-acquisition integration opportunities.
- Drive execution at scale, turning strategy into measurable commercial, operational, and financial results.
- Establish data-driven, repeatable operating frameworks that can be deployed across the portfolio.
- Influence and align CEOs, boards, and investment teams around transformation priorities.
- Report directly into Operating Partners and senior investment leadership.

Candidate Profile:

Enterprise-Level Operating Leader (Non-Negotiable)
- Experience reporting directly to a CEO, Group CEO, or Board.
- Track record of leading transformation across $1B+ businesses or complex, multi-entity portfolios.

Strategic Operator With Execution DNA
- Not just advisory, proven ability to "work in the business" and deliver results.
- Comfortable with ambiguity, complexity, and rapid decision-making in PE environments.

Commercial + Operational Breadth
- Experience driving revenue growth, operational efficiency, and business model redesign.
- Background may include GTM, sales ops, commercial strategy, enterprise operations, corporate transformation, or P&L leadership.

Private Equity & Value Creation Experience
- Exposure to PE-backed or investor-led environments, preferably across multiple assets.
- Familiarity with value creation planning, investment theses, and exit preparation.

Analytical & Framework-Driven
- Often ex-McKinsey/Bain/BCG, though not required if they demonstrate structured thinking and board-level communication skills.

Leadership & Influence
- Credibility to influence CEOs, founders, and investors.
- Able to build alignment in high-stakes, high-velocity situations.

Flexible Engagement Style
- Comfortable operating as an interim operating partner, advisor, or embedded transformation leader, depending on the portfolio need.

Requirements:
- US-based or valid US work authorisation.
- Experience leading enterprise-wide change with direct CEO or Board access.
- Track record of measurable impact in commercial, operational, or organisational transformation.

This is a rare chance to operate at the intersection of strategy, execution, and value creation within a top-tier fund. You will shape how transformation is done across the portfolio, embedding rigour, pace, and commercial outcomes at a moment where the industry is increasingly recognising the need to work in the business to unlock value.

title: Chief Operating Officer
description:
Anchored Executive Search is helping one of our clients find a rockstar COO for a Home Decor company in Lakewood, New Jersey.

Position Overview

The Chief Operating Officer (COO) is a key executive responsible for driving operational excellence, supply chain efficiency, and cross-departmental performance for a home décor brand. This role oversees day-to-day operations, ensures strong financial and operational discipline, and builds scalable systems that support growth across product development, merchandising, production, logistics, and sales channels (e-commerce, retail, B2B).

This opportunity is with a growing Home Decor company that is currently doing just under 10M in overall revenue with 35 employees currently.

Very ambitious CEO who is well known in New Jersey. This company is one of three businesses that she owns and operates.

The (CEO) is really looking for a COO that can help her scale and that is strong when it comes to putting systems into place.

She believes her products are light years ahead in branding and product, and believes with the right COO and structure that this can be a billion dollar company.

This position would require a relocation to New Jersey, where relocation would be provided.

Generous base salary, up to 300k and is open to the idea of doing profit sharing for a rockstar candidate.

Key Responsibilities:

1. Strategic Operations & Execution
- Partner with the CEO and leadership to set and execute strategic priorities.
- Lead operational planning across departments (product development, supply chain, sales, e-commerce, and retail).
- Translate big-picture goals into measurable, executable initiatives.

2. Supply Chain & Inventory Management
- Oversee end-to-end supply chain, from sourcing to final delivery.
- Manage vendor relationships and negotiate terms to optimize costs without compromising quality.
- Implement demand forecasting and inventory planning tools to reduce stockouts and excess inventory.
- Ensure logistics and warehousing operations support fast, accurate fulfillment across all channels.

3. Financial Discipline & P&L Ownership
- Own operational budget and partner with Finance to manage cash flow and cost control.
- Analyze unit economics across product lines, channels, and regions.
- Drive margin improvement through efficiency gains and process optimization.

4. Cross-Departmental Performance Management
- Establish KPIs for product development, merchandising, production, customer service, and fulfillment.
- Lead weekly operational reviews; hold teams accountable to goals.
- Build dashboards and reporting infrastructure to provide visibility across functions.

5. Technology & Process Optimization
- Evaluate, implement, and scale operational tools (ERP, inventory, order management, analytics).
- Champion automation and workflow improvements that increase speed and reduce error.
- Lead the company through change management initiatives.

6. Sales Channel Optimization
- Work with e-commerce and retail teams to ensure operational support for channel growth.
- Support B2B strategy with fulfillment and order management capabilities tailored to wholesale partners.
- Align marketing and operations to support new product launches and promotions.
